block.create_stats.auto_transmission_clu: Automatic stress governor for a kinetic shaft line
Place it inline between a power source and the branch you want to regulate
Auto-adjusts shaft speed to keep stress inside your chosen band.
Right-click to set the stress band and min/max RPM limits
block.create_stats.calculator.tooltip: Compact calculator block for quick math and graphing
block.create_stats.calculator.tooltip.pl: Place matching blocks side by side, or sneak-place to auto-fill a 2x3 panel
block.create_stats.calculator.tooltip.sh: Sneak while placing to auto-fill a 2x3 panel; Scientific mode adds x, ^, and functions.
block.create_stats.calculator.tooltip.us: Press the face buttons for quick math, or click the display to open the full screen
block.create_stats.control_clutch.toolti: Controller-driven clutch for a kinetic shaft line
Place it inline on the branch you want a controller to start or stop
Simple pass-through clutch with controller-facing state reporting and comparator signal output.
It has no UI; right-click it to check the linked controller and current signal
block.create_stats.data_dial.tooltip: Wall-mounted gauge for live machine and Display Link values
block.create_stats.data_dial.tooltip.pla: Mount it on a sturdy machine face or switch it to Linked mode
block.create_stats.data_dial.tooltip.shi: Sneak-right-click to switch between Local and Linked mode.
block.create_stats.data_dial.tooltip.usa: Right-click to cycle metric; comparator output follows the selected reading
block.create_stats.dispatch_board.toolti: Public operations board for a station's freight manifest
Right-click a Create station first to link this dispatch board
Right-click a Create station to link the board item before placing it; sneak-right-click a placed board with a linked clipboard to relink it later
Public live-ops board for one linked station; the clipboard still handles manifest authoring while the board focuses on status and order actions.
Right-click to view the live dispatch screen; operators can dismiss or reset delivery orders from the board
block.create_stats.factory_logic_control: Branch computer that pauses linked clutch lines when watched rows go idle
Insert a machine card and a clutch link card in the same row to bind one watched branch
Best with Mechanical Press and Mechanical Mixer setups; other watched rows only use nearby belt, inventory, and dropped-item heuristics.
Right-click to open the logic screen; it starts with 3 rows and expands as you fill more
block.create_stats.freight_manifest_clip: Station-linked clipboard for train cargo planning
Right-click a Create station first to link this clipboard
Link it to a Create station first, then place it standing or wall-mounted
Right-click a linked clipboard to open and edit that station's freight manifest.
Right-click to edit the linked station manifest or manage checklist lines
block.create_stats.holographic_projector: Projects floating 3D holograms of live factory data
Place it on a solid block in a dashboard area or enable its nearby scanner
Shows live hologram stats; sneak-right-click cycles the display style.
Use a Stats Configurator for full settings, or right-click for quick status
block.create_stats.load_balancer.tooltip: Splits one driven shaft into two governed output branches
Rear is input; left and right are outputs, and merged branches are invalid
Rear shaft is the input; left and right shafts are outputs. Right-click to open controls and split one kinetic source across two branches.
Right-click to set the stress band, split ratio, and Strict or Elastic sharing
block.create_stats.mini_display.tooltip: Compact wall-mounted readout for item, fluid, boiler, and kinetic stats
Mount it on a sturdy face, or place two side by side for a 2x1 display
Compact wall-mounted stats readout; link it or pair two side by side for a wider display.
Right-click to configure local or linked readouts; hold a filter item to pin storage lines
block.create_stats.overflow_gate.tooltip: Belt-side splitter that routes filtered outputs first and overflow last
Place it on a horizontal cased belt like a Create tunnel
Prioritizes filtered and open outputs first, then sends leftovers to the chosen overflow side.
Use side filters and the top selector to define Any and Overflow routing
block.create_stats.production_detector.t: Item-flow sensor for belts, depots, and nearby inventories
Face it toward the belt or machine you want to monitor
Monitors nearby item flow and opens its stats when right-clicked.
Right-click to open live stats and read activity, throughput, and target state
block.create_stats.safety_clutch.tooltip: Self-monitoring clutch that trips before a line overstresses
Place it inline on a shaft branch so it can watch that network
Trips on overload to cut power and protect the line.
Right-click to set the trip threshold, hysteresis gap, and reset behavior
block.create_stats.smart_gearshift.toolt: Redstone preset gearbox for staged RPM changes
Put it inline on a shaft line and drive it with analog redstone 0-15
Redstone-selectable gearbox stage; level 0 passes input through and higher levels shift the shaft to preset RPMs.
A Display Link can read the active stage, target RPM, source RPM, and boost/throttle state
block.create_stats.stats_alarm.tooltip: Panel alarm that watches nearby Stats Display Panels for alert states
block.create_stats.stats_alarm.tooltip.p: Place it near a panel controller so it can scan the display network
block.create_stats.stats_alarm.tooltip.s: Scans nearby Stats Display Panels and turns on when any channel reaches its alert threshold.
block.create_stats.stats_alarm.tooltip.u: Use the Stats Configurator to set radius, sound, visibility, and redstone output
block.create_stats.stats_display_panel.t: Wall-mounted live display for factory data, graphs, and dashboards
Place matching panels side by side to form a larger multiblock screen
Sneak-right-click to cycle the display mode.
Right-click to open the panel; use a Stats Configurator to copy or paste settings
block.create_stats.stats_recorder.source: Nearby Stats Display - waiting for data
block.create_stats.stats_recorder.toolti: Records incoming Display Link samples for later review
Use it as a Display Link target anywhere you want to log live values
Records Display Link data and copies stored samples into a Memory Cog.
Right-click with empty hand for status, or use a Memory Cog to copy stored samples
block.create_stats.status_tower_light.to: Compact operator light for local or linked machine status
Place it above a machine, or feed it with a Display Link for remote status
Compact status light for nearby or linked machine readouts; green, yellow, and red show normal, warning, and critical states.
Right-click to set source mode, metric, thresholds, and alert logic
create_stats.efficiency.no_data: Connect a Stressometer to grade this network
create_stats.hologram.no_data: No Data — Connect a Display Link
create_stats.manual.page.aero_flight_dec: The "Is that a ship?" page is the large-display overview for ships. It combines engine, motion, trim, docking, navigation, lift, and force data on one screen. The top row shows which systems are reporting. The center keeps speed, attitude, and trim cues easy to read. Use it when you want the whole craft at a glance. Switch back to a specialized page when you need one source in detail.
create_stats.manual.page.aero_source_mat: Flight sources cover the blocks you check most while testing a ship. Engines report burn and output. Velocity, Gimbal, and Altitude Sensors report motion, attitude, and height. Docking Connectors, Steering Wheels, and Navigation Tables report guidance and control. Laser Sensors, Optical Sensors, Linked Typewriters, and Auger Shafts can also feed data. Hot Air Burners and Steam Vents add lifting-gas telemetry when Aeronautics is present.
create_stats.manual.page.aero_stats_link: With Simulated or Aeronautics installed, several flight blocks can act as Stats Link sources. Put a Display Link on the source and choose Stats Link on the source side. Then send that data to panels, projectors, recorders, dials, lights, or any other linked target. Use it to keep ship data visible while you fly or tune. A linked Gimbal Sensor also unlocks Contraption Info.
create_stats.manual.page.aeroline_direct: Aeroline Director is the guidance page for a flight bridge. It combines gimbal attitude with navigation, docking, helm, and velocity data, then turns that into one pilot display. The page only appears when the panel sees a Gimbal Sensor and at least one guidance source, such as a Navigation Table, Docking Connector, or Steering Wheel. Use it when the display should answer what to do next, not just what the ship is doing.
create_stats.manual.page.auto_transmissi: Place the Auto Variable Clutch inline on a kinetic line to turn it into an automatic stress governor. Open its UI to set a stress band in SU or percent and define min and max RPM, then connect it between a power source and your machines. The clutch watches the network and changes ratio on its own: it speeds up when stress is below target and slows down when stress rises above it. If the network drops out, it holds neutral until power returns.
create_stats.manual.page.calculator.body: The Calculator is a compact math block for quick counting and graphing. Press the face buttons to build an expression, use C or backspace to edit, and click the display to open the full calculator screen. It keeps your current expression, shows the latest result or error, and stores recent calculations. Sneak-placing a matching Calculator can auto-fill a 2x3 panel, and Scientific mode unlocks x, powers, pi/e, and functions for graphing and advanced formulas.
create_stats.manual.page.contraption_inf: Contraption Info is a second source unlocked by a linked Gimbal Sensor on an assembled Simulated ship. It focuses on force balance instead of raw attitude. Vertical Margin is support minus weight. Forward Margin is thrust minus drag. Support combines levitation, balloon buoyancy, and aerodynamic lift. Watch the margins first, then mass and state, when you need to know whether the craft can climb, hold trim, or push forward.
The Control Clutch is the branch clutch for the Factory Logic Controller. Place it in the shaft line you want a controller row to stop or restart. Link it with a Clutch Link Card, then insert that card into the controller. The clutch does not tune stress or speed on its own. It only follows RUN and STOP from its linked row. Right-click for link details, and use the comparator or redstone output when you need status outside the menu.
create_stats.manual.page.data_dial.body: The Data Dial is a wall-mounted gauge for live machine readouts. Place it against a sturdy face to read the block behind it in Local mode, or switch it to Linked mode to follow values sent in from a Display Link. Right-click cycles the metric being shown, and sneak-right-click swaps the mode. The needle and comparator output both follow the selected reading, so it works as both a visual dial and a compact analog signal source.
The Dispatch Board is a public operations display for a freight station. First right-click a Create station with the board item to bind it, then place it standing on the ground or mounted on a wall near the linked platform. Right-click the placed board to open the live dispatch screen. The board shows cargo totals, delivery-order status, and train presence at a glance. Operators with permission can hide, reveal, or reset delivery orders from the board, while the Freight Manifest Clipboard remains the tool for full manifest authoring. You can also sneak-right-click a placed board with a linked clipboard to relink it or copy the board link back into the clipboard item.
create_stats.manual.page.factory_logic_c: The Factory Logic Controller stops a branch when its machines sit idle for too long. Each row needs a Machine Selection Card for the watched area and a Clutch Link Card for the branch clutch. Set the idle time with the +/- buttons. Pause keeps a row off until Reset. When work resumes, the controller sends RUN again. It is built for Control Clutches, and works best with presses and mixers.
create_stats.manual.page.freight_manifes: The Freight Manifest Clipboard is a station-linked notice board for train cargo planning. First right-click a Create station with the clipboard item to bind it to that station, then place the block standing on the ground or mounted on a wall. Once linked, right-click the clipboard to open the station's freight manifest and manage the cargo checklist for that route. You can also use a linked clipboard item to relink the placed block or copy the block's link back into your hand.
create_stats.manual.page.holographic_pro: The Holographic Projector is a central stats display for your factory. Feed it data with a Display Link, or enable the nearby scanner to pick up supported blocks like gauges, tanks, switches, and stations around it. It renders the selected channels as floating holograms, can be tuned with the Stats Configurator, and can also emit redstone based on a chosen channel. Place it on a solid block in a control room or dashboard area where you want live readouts at a glance.
create_stats.manual.page.hot_air_burner_: Hot Air Burners and Steam Vents share one lifting-gas telemetry view. It shows signal strength, live output, target volume, capacity, gas type, flow state, fill state, and delivered lift. That is the short list you need to tune a balloon system quickly. Use a Data Dial or Status Tower Light for one number, or feed this source into a larger bridge display when gas output matters.
create_stats.manual.page.intro.body: This manual gives the quick version. Pick a page on the left to see what a block does, how to use it, and when it helps. If a page spans more than one sheet, use the arrows on the right page to flip through the rest.
create_stats.manual.page.load_balancer.b: The Load Balancer is a split-shaft kinetic block for dividing one driven shaft into two output lines. Place it so the rear face receives power; the left and right faces feed the two branches, and right-click to open its controls. Set a stress band, choose how the load is split, and pick Strict or Elastic sharing. In Strict mode each branch follows its own share; in Elastic mode it can borrow extra headroom when one side needs more. The block reports when the input is missing, the topology is invalid, or the branches have been merged.
create_stats.manual.page.machine_selecti: Use the Machine Selection Card to tell one controller row what to watch. Sneak-right-click a machine to store its type. Then click two positions in the same dimension to mark the watched area. Using the card again after both corners are set starts a new selection. Hold the card to preview the hovered machine or saved box. Put it in the same controller row as the matching Clutch Link Card.
create_stats.manual.page.mini_display.bo: The Mini Display is a compact wall-mounted readout for machine stats. Place it on a sturdy face to read the machine beneath it, or switch it to linked mode to relay a Display Link source instead. It can show item storage, fluids, boiler info, and kinetic data, and two Mini Displays placed side by side merge into a wider 2x1 display. Right-click it to configure what it tracks, and for item storage you can hold a filter item while configuring to pin a specific item.
create_stats.manual.page.overflow_gate.b: The Overflow Gate is a belt splitter for Create logistics. Place it above a moving belt to sort one buffered stack across the three side outputs around the belt. It sends items to matching filtered sides first, then to any open side, and only then to the chosen overflow side, so you can keep a main line moving while diverting extra items where you want them. Right-click to check its current routing, and sneak-right-click to take back anything waiting in the buffer.
create_stats.manual.page.production_dete: The Production Detector is an item-flow sensor for belts and inventories. Face it toward the belt or machine you want to watch, then right-click it to open the live stats screen. It counts items passing through the target, shows current throughput, and can output redstone based on activity. For belts it reads the adjacent belt line; for other inventories it scans forward to find the first valid target in range. Use it when you want a simple way to measure output, spot jams, or feed item-flow data into a Create setup.
create_stats.manual.page.safety_clutch.b: The Safety Clutch is a kinetic safety valve for Create networks. Place it inline on a shaft line to monitor the stress flowing through that network. When the network reaches its set stress threshold, or goes into overload, the clutch trips and cuts power to protect the machinery downstream. Open its settings with right-click to choose the trip threshold, hysteresis, and whether it stays latched or automatically resets after the load drops. Use it anywhere you want a machine line to fail safe instead of burning out under pressure.
create_stats.manual.page.safety_clutch_l: Use the Clutch Link Card to tell one controller row which clutch it can command. Right-click a Control Clutch to store its position, then place the card in the same row as the matching Machine Selection Card. Sneak-right-click in the air to clear it. Legacy Safety Clutches still work, but the normal setup is Factory Logic Controller plus Machine Selection Card plus Control Clutch.
create_stats.manual.page.smart_gearshift: Smart Gearshift is a redstone preset gearbox for kinetic shafts. Each redstone level from 0 to 15 selects a speed stage: level 0 passes input through, while higher levels convert the incoming rotation to one of 15 target RPMs from 8 up to 256. It can boost or throttle to reach the chosen stage, but it never exceeds 256 RPM. Place it inline on a kinetic line and power it with redstone to change speeds on the fly. A Display Link can show the current stage, target RPM, source RPM, and whether the block is boosting or throttling.
create_stats.manual.page.stats_alarm.bod: Place the Stats Alarm near a Stats Display Panel controller to watch your network for trouble. It scans nearby panels for any active channel that reaches its alert threshold, then turns on, plays a configurable sound, can spawn particles, and may show a warning message. Use the Stats Configurator to adjust scan radius, sound, visibility, and whether the alarm also powers redstone.
create_stats.manual.page.stats_display_p: The Stats Display Panel is a wall-mounted display for live factory data. Right-click it to open the panel UI and view the current graph or dashboard; sneak-right-click to cycle the display mode. When multiple panels face the same direction, they can form a larger multiblock display with one controller. Use a Stats Configurator to copy or paste settings, and hold Shift while placing panels to auto-fill matching neighbors when you want to build a larger screen quickly.
The Stats Recorder is the capture box for your factory instruments. It can still record data sent in from a Display Link, but it also arms itself when placed near a Stats Display and mirrors that panel's main channel automatically. Right-click with an empty hand to see whether it is armed, recording, paused, or waiting for a source. Hold an empty Memory Cog and interact with the recorder to copy the stored samples into a portable snapshot.
create_stats.manual.page.status_tower_li: The Status Tower Light is a compact operator lamp for machine status. Place it above a compatible machine for local monitoring, or link it to a remote source with a Display Link. Configure it with right-click to choose the source mode, metric, and whether the light uses warning thresholds or simple on-off logic. Green means normal, yellow means warning or no data, and red means critical; the critical state also emits redstone power.
create_stats.overflow_gate.tip.set_rule: Hold to set Any or Overflow
create_stats.prediction.tooltip: Show predicted trend lines with confidence bands
create_stats.tooltip.configurator: Right-click a Stats Display Panel to configure it
create_stats.tooltip.handheld: Right-click to open a pocket stats terminal
create_stats.tooltip.handheld.memory_cog: Hold a Memory Cog in your other hand to load stored data
create_stats.tooltip.holographic_project: Projects a floating 3D hologram of live statistics
create_stats.tooltip.memory_cog.empty: Empty — use a Stats Recorder to write data
gui.create_stats.factory_manual.scroll_h: Use the wheel, arrow keys, or buttons to browse
gui.create_stats.factory_manual.subtitle: Create Stats field handbook
gui.create_stats.handheld.title: Stats Handheld
item.create_stats.factory_manual.tooltip: Browse every Create Stats block in one in-game handbook
item.create_stats.memory_cog.tooltip.emp: Empty - use a Stats Recorder to write data
item.create_stats.memory_cog.tooltip.vie: Use with the Stats Handheld to inspect the snapshot
item.create_stats.stats_handheld.tooltip: Sneak-right-click in air to clear the remote link
Use on a Stats Display, Stats Recorder, or Dispatch Board to link it
Hold a Memory Cog in the other hand for local snapshot playback
Use it on a Stats Display, Stats Recorder, or Dispatch Board to link a remote terminal view
message.create_stats.stats_recorder.memo: [Stats Recorder] This Memory Cog already contains data.
message.create_stats.stats_recorder.no_d: [Stats Recorder] No data recorded yet - connect a Display Link or place it near a Stats Display first!
message.create_stats.stats_recorder.writ: [Stats Recorder] Memory Cog written with %s samples!
